Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Slack for April incident and damage discussions using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "Mosaic" or "damage" or "prototype" (or similar), filtered to April 2026. Expected findings: messages about the Zara Okoye prototype damage ($90K value, cracked servo motor housing), Blessing Okafor signing off on crating remotely, Heartland Movers involvement, DOT compliance discussions, NorthWind chain-of-custody breach, Emilia Cruz piano scratch, and coordinator capacity warnings.
OE 2: Search Slack for overload and capacity warnings using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "overload" or "capacity" or "coordinator" (or similar), filtered to April 2026. Expected findings: Emeka Diallo flagging overload around April 5 (carrying five active relocations across four clients -- Sunbelt VP, Palmetto Community Foundation, Axiom Precision Manufacturing, Mosaic Robotics), Chloe Vance acknowledging the flag, Catalina Dubois's capacity being flagged as a concern around April 13-15 (David Chen emailed Elena requesting redistribution of Catalina's accounts via email_email_15467c2ea643, citing NorthWind chain-of-custody breach, piano damage, and Catalina's broken April 14 recovery promise to Pam Kowalski; Catalina also drafted but did not send an email to David acknowledging she was "underwater on NorthWind"), and Chloe flagging coordinator capacity crisis to executive leadership around April 24-25.
OE 3: Search Slack for DOT compliance and paperwork discussions using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "DOT" or "paperwork" or "placarding" (or similar). Expected findings: messages about the DOT hazmat audit, Keiko Tanaka laser cutter missing placarding certificate, Blessing Okafor having received verbal confirmation only from Swift driver, and the $50K+ fine exposure.
OE 4: Search emails for April client complaints, damage reports, and escalations using search_emails with queries related to "damage" or "prototype" or "Mosaic" or "NorthWind" (or similar). Expected findings: Tamara Byrd demanding incident report for Mosaic prototype damage, Victor Huang sending furious security breach report about Raj Patel server lab, Pam Kowalski formal final warning to David Chen mentioning PathFinder Logistics competitor, and internal escalation threads.
OE 5: Search emails for Elena's response to the account redistribution request using search_emails with queries related to "rebalance" or "redistribute" or "Catalina" or "accounts" (or similar). Expected finding: Elena Rostova's email (email_id: email_email_ccaaecb4e47a) to david.chen@moveops.com, CC catalina.dubois@moveops.com, subject "Re: Need approval to rebalance Catalina's accounts", containing: "Please handle this within the team for now. I am not approving account reassignments or additional headcount this week."
OE 6: Search Slack for BrightLoop expansion and coordinator hire discussions using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "BrightLoop" or "coordinator hire" or "temp coordinator" (or similar). Expected findings: David Chen and Chloe Vance requesting temp coordinator authorization for 4-6 weeks to handle BrightLoop May wave, and Elena not having responded to this request.
OE 7: List Airtable relocation records using airtable_list_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001" and table_name "Relocations" to map coordinator assignments across all April moves. Expected findings: Blessing Okafor assigned to relocations across Canopy Health (Dr. Yusuf Abdi, Lily Marchetti, Kevin Okafor, Marcus Webb Detroit-Chicago), Mosaic Robotics (Zara Okoye), NorthWind Technologies (Raj Patel, Emilia Cruz), BrightLoop Analytics (Preethi Nair), PivotPoint Labs (Ines Kovac), and more. Suki Patel assigned to relocations across BrightLoop (Jordan Ekwueme, Simone Richter, Marcus Webb Atlanta-Boston), Mosaic (Andre Washington, Deshawn Hartley, Andre Mitchell), Vectral (Daniel Reeves, Maya Johansson, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya), StormCloud (Jae-won Kim), and Sunbelt (Daniela Cruz). This confirms Blessing was carrying 10 concurrent April relocations across 6 clients (Canopy Health, Mosaic Robotics, NorthWind Technologies, BrightLoop Analytics, PivotPoint Labs, and Tideway Hospitality Tech) and Suki was carrying 12 concurrent April relocations across 5 clients.
OE 8: Search Linear for DOT compliance tickets using linear_list_issues with queries related to "DOT" or "compliance" or "hazmat" or "placarding" (or similar). Expected findings: DOT compliance audit project with tickets for Keiko Tanaka (issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic, priority 1, state in_progress, assigned to Chloe Vance, description names Blessing Okafor as coordinator and attributes the missing placarding certificate to her -- note: the Airtable relocation record recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle lists Javier Morales as assigned coordinator, creating a cross-system inconsistency the agent may encounter), and the subtask to obtain retroactive certificate from Swift (issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert).
OE 9: Search Linear for overload and capacity tickets using linear_list_issues with queries related to "overload" or "rebalance" or "coverage" or "Emeka" (or similar). Expected finding: Emeka's overload rebalancing ticket (linear_issue_644c3b2e75a6, title "Rebalance April account coverage for Emeka across Sunbelt, Palmetto, Axiom, and Mosaic", created April 5, assigned to Emeka Diallo, no resolution -- ticket has no due date and no state change since creation, confirming the overload was flagged but never acted on).
OE 10: Search Linear for NorthWind, Mosaic damage, and StormCloud related tickets using linear_list_issues with queries related to "NorthWind" or "retention" or "StormCloud" or "damage" (or similar). Expected findings: NorthWind retention response plan ticket (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1, priority 1, assigned to David Chen), StormCloud incident reconstruction ticket (linear_issue_a49b4d199139, assigned to Mina Hashimoto, describing the chain of failures including Suki Patel dropping Jae-won Kim's lease follow-up -- note: the ticket description itself does not attribute the lapse to Deshawn Hartley's trade show deadline; that causal link is discoverable separately via Slack messages or persona context).
OE 11: Search QuickBooks for bills and accruals related to damage claims, DOT penalties, and insurance using quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll set to true. Expected findings: Mosaic prototype damage contingent liability accrual (bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001, $90,000, vendor Heartland Movers), DOT penalty accrual (bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2, $50,000, vendor U.S. DOT - PHMSA Hazardous Materials Division), and KeyMove piano insurance claim rider (BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417, $1,200, vendor KeyMove Specialty Transport, description references Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch during stairwell extraction).
OE 12: Search CRM for affected client companies using crm_search_companies with queries for "Mosaic" (or similar). Expected finding: company_mosaic (Mosaic Robotics). Repeat for "NorthWind" to find company_northwind, "StormCloud" to find company_stormcloud, "BrightLoop" to find company_brightloop, "Sunbelt" to find company_sunbelt, and "Palmetto" to find crm_company_69f1ae00a4b9 (Palmetto Community Foundation). These company IDs are needed for CRM engagement updates.
OE 13: Search CRM for deals associated with affected clients using crm_search_deals with company_id for each affected company. Expected findings: Mosaic deals including the damage claim risk deal (deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk, $2,700, description contains "RISK FLAG -- PROTOTYPE DAMAGE INCIDENT" and names Blessing Okafor as coordinator). NorthWind deals including the Denver expansion (deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2, $14,700, contractSent stage). StormCloud won deal (deal_stormcloud_won, $26,400, closedWon stage, company_id is null -- agent must search by dealname "StormCloud" rather than by company_id). BrightLoop deals including April relocations (deal_brightloop_apr2026, $11,350).
OE 14: Look up David Chen's contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "David Chen" (or similar). Expected finding: david.chen@moveops.com.
OE 15: Look up Chloe Vance's contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Chloe Vance" (or similar). Expected finding: chloe.vance@moveops.com.
OE 16: Update CRM for Mosaic Robotics by creating an engagement note using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids ["company_mosaic"]. Content should cover: coordinator overload root cause analysis, Blessing Okafor involved in prototype damage (remote crating sign-off while in Philadelphia for Canopy move) and DOT placarding gap (juggling multiple moves same week), financial exposure ($90,000 contingent liability, $50,000 DOT penalty accrual, $40,000 insurance gap), and account risk status.
OE 17: Update CRM for NorthWind Technologies by creating an engagement note using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids ["company_northwind"]. Content should cover: Blessing Okafor as coordinator for both Raj Patel (chain-of-custody breach -- movers arrived before IT escort, overnight warehouse storage) and Emilia Cruz (piano damage during extraction, $1,200 KeyMove claim), Catalina Dubois promised recovery plan April 14 never delivered, Pam Kowalski bypassed Catalina with formal final warning mentioning PathFinder Logistics competitor, $14,700 invoice at risk.
OE 18: Update CRM for StormCloud Analytics by creating an engagement note using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids ["company_stormcloud"]. Content should cover: Suki Patel dropped Jae-won Kim guarantor packet follow-up (promised April 9 in response to Carmen Reyes's April 8 flag, never sent), Airtable record was previously marked Completed incorrectly which caused Omar Ibrahim to deprioritize the follow-up as low-priority (Linear ticket linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0 confirms this), record since corrected to In Progress by Chloe Vance on April 24 but lease remains unsigned, overload root cause (Suki was simultaneously managing 11+ relocations across multiple clients), $26,400 revenue at risk.
OE 19: Update CRM for BrightLoop Analytics by creating an engagement note using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids ["company_brightloop"]. Content should cover: Blessing Okafor missed Road Runner Auto Transport delay notice for Marcus Webb Honda Civic while buried in NorthWind and Mosaic handoffs, Simone Richter apartment mismatch (studio vs 1BR) unanswered for over two weeks (Mina replied April 9 promising follow-up by end of week but never followed through; Julian Brooks sent the substantive client-facing response ~April 24 after taking over BrightLoop service recovery), $11,350 current deal at risk, May expansion wave incoming with no coordinator capacity.
OE 20: Update CRM for Sunbelt Venture Partners by creating an engagement note using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids ["company_sunbelt"]. Content should cover: Emeka Diallo overload led to delayed response during Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap incident, Hana Kim sent wrong PDF attachments exposing nonprofit pricing to for-profit client, capacity-related context (Emeka flagged overload April 5 but no redistribution occurred).
OE 21: Update CRM for Palmetto Community Foundation by creating an engagement note using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids ["crm_company_69f1ae00a4b9"]. Content should cover: received wrong invoice PDF during April 10 batch send (got Sunbelt's commercial-rate invoice instead of own nonprofit invoice), nonprofit with audit-sensitive documentation requirements, Emeka Diallo overload contributing context (flagged April 5, never resolved, went quiet during incident), need for corrected documentation and clean audit trail.
OE 22: Message David Chen with findings using send_email from elena.rostova@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com (or alternatively using conversations_add_message to a channel David can see such as C002 #customer-engagement or C004 #executive). Content must include: which coordinators and AMs appear across multiple incidents (Blessing Okafor in 4+ incidents across Mosaic/NorthWind/BrightLoop, Suki Patel in StormCloud/BrightLoop, Catalina Dubois NorthWind pattern, Emeka Diallo overload flag), specific failures per person, early warnings raised and by whom (Emeka April 5, Catalina April 10-13, David April 21-22, Chloe April 24-25), verified financial costs from QuickBooks ($90,000 + $50,000 + $1,200 = $141,200 in accrued costs plus revenue at risk across accounts), and the hire-and-redistribute recommendation.
OE 23: Message Chloe Vance with findings using send_email from elena.rostova@moveops.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com (or alternatively using conversations_add_message to C006 #operations or C004 #executive). Content must include: the same coordinator pattern findings, Airtable-verified load data showing Blessing at 10 and Suki at 12 concurrent April relocations, early warnings timeline, verified financial exposure, and the hire-and-redistribute recommendation with specific ask for Chloe's capacity assessment.
OE 24: Report the final analysis to Elena in the agent's response. This is a response-content step (not a tool call) that drives Outcome 2.1 rubrics. The response must cover: the determination that this is a capacity problem not a people problem, the person-by-person failure map with specific incidents, the early warning chain and who did or did not act (including Elena's own redistribution denial email and non-response to BrightLoop coordinator hire request), the verified QuickBooks financial totals ($90,000 Mosaic accrual + $50,000 DOT accrual + $1,200 KeyMove piano claim), revenue at risk per affected account, and the recommendation to both hire and redistribute.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

OE 16: Update CRM for Mosaic Robotics by creating an engagement note using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids ["company_mosaic"]. Content should cover: Blessing Okafor as coordinator for both Raj Patel (chain-of-custody breach -- movers arrived before IT escort, overnight warehouse storage) and Emilia Cruz (piano damage during extraction, $1,200 KeyMove claim), Catalina Dubois promised recovery plan April 14 never delivered, Pam Kowalski bypassed Catalina with formal final warning mentioning PathFinder Logistics competitor, $14,700 invoice at risk.
OE 18: Update CRM for StormCloud Analytics by creating an engagement note using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids ["company_stormcloud"]. Content should cover: Suki Patel dropped Jae-won Kim guarantor packet follow-up (promised April 9 in response to Carmen Reyes's April 8 flag, never sent), Airtable record was previously marked Completed incorrectly which caused Omar Ibrahim to deprioritize the follow-up as low-priority (Linear ticket linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0 confirms this), record since corrected to In Progress by Chloe Vance on April 24 but lease remains unsigned, overload root cause (Suki was simultaneously managing 11+ relocations across multiple clients), $26,400 revenue at risk.
OE 19: Update CRM for BrightLoop Analytics by creating an engagement note using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids ["company_brightloop"]. Content should cover: Blessing Okafor missed Road Runner Auto Transport delay notice for Marcus Webb Honda Civic while buried in NorthWind and Mosaic handoffs, Simone Richter apartment mismatch (studio vs 1BR) unanswered for over two weeks (Mina replied April 9 promising follow-up by end of week but never followed through; Julian Brooks sent the substantive client-facing response ~April 24 after taking over BrightLoop service recovery), $11,350 current deal at risk, May expansion wave incoming with no coordinator capacity.
OE 20: Update CRM for Sunbelt Venture Partners by creating an engagement note using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids ["company_sunbelt"]. Content should cover: Emeka Diallo overload led to delayed response during Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap incident, Hana Kim sent wrong PDF attachments exposing nonprofit pricing to for-profit client, capacity-related context (Emeka flagged overload April 5 but no redistribution occurred).
OE 21: Update CRM for Palmetto Community Foundation by creating an engagement note using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids ["crm_company_69f1ae00a4b9"]. Content should cover: received wrong invoice PDF during April 10 batch send (got Sunbelt's commercial-rate invoice instead of own nonprofit invoice), nonprofit with audit-sensitive documentation requirements, Emeka Diallo overload contributing context (flagged April 5, never resolved, went quiet during incident), need for corrected documentation and clean audit trail.
OE 22: Message David Chen with findings using send_email from elena.rostova@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com (or alternatively using conversations_add_message to a channel David can see such as C002 #customer-engagement or C004 #executive). Content must include: which coordinators and AMs appear across multiple incidents (Blessing Okafor in 4+ incidents across Mosaic/NorthWind/BrightLoop, Suki Patel in StormCloud/BrightLoop, Catalina Dubois NorthWind pattern, Emeka Diallo overload flag), specific failures per person, early warnings raised and by whom (Emeka April 5, Catalina April 10-13, David April 21-22, Chloe April 24-25), verified financial costs from QuickBooks ($90,000 + $50,000 + $1,200 = $141,200 in accrued costs plus revenue at risk across accounts), and the hire-and-redistribute recommendation.
OE 23: Message Chloe Vance with findings using send_email from elena.rostova@moveops.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com (or alternatively using conversations_add_message to C006 #operations or C004 #executive). Content must include: the same coordinator pattern findings, Airtable-verified load data showing Blessing at 10 and Suki at 12 concurrent April relocations, early warnings timeline, verified financial exposure, and the hire-and-redistribute recommendation with specific ask for Chloe's capacity assessment.
OE 24: Report the final analysis to Elena in the agent's response. This is a response-content step (not a tool call) that drives Outcome 2.1 rubrics. The response must cover: the determination that this is a capacity problem not a people problem, the person-by-person failure map with specific incidents, the early warning chain and who did or did not act (including Elena's own redistribution denial email and non-response to BrightLoop coordinator hire request), the verified QuickBooks financial totals ($90,000 Mosaic accrual + $50,000 DOT accrual + $1,200 KeyMove piano claim), revenue at risk per affected account, and the recommendation to both hire and redistribute.

outcome

✓Run #5The final response explicitly states 'Hire AND redistribute' as the recommendation. The message to David and Chloe asks about both 'redistributed' and 'net-new headcount' simultaneously, making clear that both actions are required.
✓Run #6The final response has a dedicated section 'THE ANSWER: You Need to Both Hire AND Redistribute' with explicit rationale: redistribution alone won't suffice and hiring alone is too slow. Decisions list both temp coordinator hire AND immediate Blessing load redistribution.

No email with subject about 'rebalance' or Catalina's accounts was found or referenced.
✗Run #6While the final response acknowledges 'leadership (including you)' didn't act fast enough, it never specifically identifies Elena Rostova as having denied an account redistribution request or references her explicit email denial ('I am not approving account reassignments or additional headcount this week'). The agent did not search emails for 'rebalance' or 'redistribute' terms that would have surfaced this email, so the specific accountability finding about Elena's denial was missed.
24 The model must use a QuickBooks tool (e.g., quickbooks_search_bills, quickbooks_get_bill, or quickbooks_show_data) to retrieve financial data.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✗ 83%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows quickbooks_search_bills was called with {fetchAll: true} and returned results (output_file referenced). This satisfies the requirement to use a QuickBooks tool to retrieve financial data.
✓Run #2quickbooks_search_bills was called with {fetchAll: true} and returned 17 bills including the three key accruals (bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 at $90K, bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2 at $50K, and BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 at $1,200). The agent used the QuickBooks tool to retrieve real financial data.
✓Run #3quickbooks_search_bills was called with {fetchAll: true} and successfully returned 17 bills including bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 ($90K), bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2 ($50K), and BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 ($1,200). This is a direct QuickBooks tool call for financial data retrieval.
✓Run #4The agent called quickbooks_search_invoices multiple times (for INV-2026-0411, INV-2026-0412, by CustomerRef.name for NorthWind, StormCloud, Sunbelt, Palmetto) to retrieve financial data from QuickBooks.
✓Run #5The agent called quickbooks_search_invoices multiple times (for INV-2026-0411, INV-2026-0412, INV-2026-0319, and by CustomerRef.name for Sunbelt, Palmetto, StormCloud, Terraform Digital) and also called quickbooks_search_bills (which errored). QuickBooks tools were clearly used.
✗Run #6The agent used quickbooks_search_invoices multiple times but never called quickbooks_search_bills, quickbooks_get_bill, or quickbooks_show_data. The $90K accrual and $50K DOT accrual are bills, not invoices, and would only be found via bills queries. The agent missed these authoritative bill records.
